summary: local-mcp ships install/postinstall code that beacons system info to a non-publisher Railway endpoint
details: The package's lifecycle/setup code (download.js, index.js, setup.js) imports child_process, fs, os, http, and https together, reads os.homedir() and process.env, and performs HTTP/HTTPS calls to a hardcoded endpoint at https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app. This Railway-hosted host does not match the package's stated publisher domains (download.local-mcp.com / local-mcp.com) and is the canonical shape of an install-time C2 beacon: combine OS/identity/env reads with outbound POST/GET to an unrelated, opaque PaaS host. setup.js additionally contains multiple POST sites paired with https require()s, consistent with exfiltration of host data and/or fetch-and-execute of remote payloads at install. The mismatch between the package's own download infrastructure (local-mcp.com) and the hardcoded Railway endpoint, together with environment/home-directory reads being shipped over HTTPS to that endpoint, is unambiguous attacker-controlled traffic on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
